Move your cursor over any yellow text to learn more about the game concept - even from [inside other tooltips!|TOOLTIPS]

AtG is a turn-based game. Each turn, [Resources|RESOURCE] are [Produced|PRODUCE] and [Consumed|CONSUME], and [Units|UNIT] may [Move|MOVE-POINT] a certain distance. Once all possible actions have been taken the player must end their turn and allow the other (computer) players to make their moves.
like other Resources, but any surplus is converted and added to your Stockpile as 'Stored Turns'. You gain
when Producing more Food than you're Consuming, and lose them when the opposite is the case.[BLANK-LINE]Each turn that passes your Tribe consumes exactly 1 Stored Turn of Food.

There are 2 [ways to win|WINNING] in AtG:[BLANK-LINE][*] Conquering the [Capital City|CAPITAL] of one of the two [Roman Empire Factions|ROME].[BLANK-LINE][*] Becoming 'Magister Militum' and taking over [Rome|ROME] from within by sending five [Clans|CLAN] to become Roman Legions.[BLANK-LINE]Note that there are two Roman [Factions|FACTION]: the Eastern and Western halves of the previously-whole-in-better-days Roman Empire, and either one can satisfy your [Victory Conditions|WINNING].[BLANK-LINE]Conquering a Roman capital will require locating it on the map and significant military preparation. Although various setbacks will befall the Romans they start off quite strong and can put up quite a fight.[BLANK-LINE]Becoming Magister Militum is more of an economic victory, as to send a [Clan|CLAN] to [Rome|ROME] not only means sending it off for a year but also providing it with expensive, late-game equipment.

Caravans provide an opportunity to exchange [Resources|RESOURCE] you don't need for those you do.[BLANK-LINE]Caravans visit 3 times per year, in Late March, June, and September. Note that it does NOT  show up during the winter, so plan accordingly![BLANK-LINE]You can visit a Caravan by clicking the button at the top of the screen (after its arrived for the first time), clicking the Notification icon announcing its arrival, or presssing [HOTKEY:F2].

There are two distinct and independently-run Roman Empires. Both are quite strong in the early going, and picking a fight with them is very dangerous. In time you'll grow much stronger and the Romans weaker, but for now it's probably best to stay on their good side.[BLANK-LINE]Your [ultimate goal|WINNING] in AtG is to defeat the Romans by capturing the [Capital City|CAPITAL] of either half of the Empire, or take the empire over from within by becoming Magister Militum.
Bandits aren't very smart, but they're reckless brutes who love killing and [Pillaging|PILLAGE]. Make sure you protect your defenseless
from them! You can recognize them by their black flag.

In addition to the major [Factions|FACTION] there are also a number of [Structures|STRUCTURE] owned independently by local people. They mind their own business, but their Structures and [Clans|CLAN] can be captured by a [Warrior|WARRIOR], should you defeat whatever defender is present.

Factions are the different kinds of tribes, kingdoms, and empires to be found throughout the world.[BLANK-LINE]Different Factions all start in different situations, and are at different level of advancement, except for the human player, who always starts with a single [SETTLEMENT] and 3 [Clans|CLAN].[BLANK-LINE]You start the game only able to play as the Goths, but more can be unlocked by conquering the [Capital|CAPITAL] of another Faction, or forming an [Alliance|ALLIANCE] with it.[BLANK-LINE]Special, non-playable Factions include the [Romans|ROME], the [Bandits|BANDIT], and [Neutral|NEUTRAL] minor tribes.

Each major [Faction|FACTION] has one or two special rules which make it play differently from the others. The Goths start with extra [FOOD] and [TREASURE], whereas the Huns start with a [HORSE-ARCHER] but can't own [Structures|STRUCTURE].

Every playable [Faction|FACTION] Leader has a unique [Personality|LEADER-TRAIT] which greatly shapes their behavior, particularly their Approach towards you.[BLANK-LINE]Refer to the 'Diplomacy' tip in the Help Screen ([HOTKEY:?]) for more information.
Each Leader has two 'Personality Traits', which helps shape which diplomatic [Approaches|APPROACH] and [Interactions|INTERACTION] they choose.

Borders, or Control, represents the [Tiles|TILE] which a player has direct ownership over and appears as a solid, colored line on the map.[BLANK-LINE]Control is very important, as within it your [Units|UNIT] always have full [Supply|SUPPLY], and it allows [Structures|STRUCTURE] to be [Constructed|CONSTRUCT], which far exceed foraging in their economic ouput.[BLANK-LINE]Control can be expanded by spending [WEAPONS] to [Fortify|FORTIFY] a Structure. [WATCHTOWER:S] are the one type of Structure which can be built outside of your Control, giving you the ability to project your power at a distance, should you be willing to make the investment.

Borders, or Control, represents the [Tiles|TILE] which a player has direct ownership over and appears as a solid, colored line on the map.[BLANK-LINE]Control is very important, as within it your [Units|UNIT] always have full [Supply|SUPPLY], and it allows [Structures|STRUCTURE] to be [Constructed|CONSTRUCT], which far exceed foraging in their economic ouput.[BLANK-LINE]Control can be expanded by spending [WEAPONS] to [Fortify|FORTIFY] a Structure. [WATCHTOWER:S] are the one type of Structure which can be built outside of your Control, giving you the ability to project your power at a distance, should you be willing to make the investment.
Maps in AtG are divided into a grid of hex tiles.[BLANK-LINE]You can show or hide the grid by pressing [HOTKEY:G], and pressing [HOTKEY:1] will make it darker or lighter.

Each [Tile|TILE] is comprised of at least one type of Terrain and some can have up to four or five.[BLANK-LINE][FERTILE:S], [DRY:S], and [DESERT:S] are 'base' Terrain types which can be found by themselves. A Tile can also have [HILL:S] or vegetation like a [Forest|FOREST] or [BRUSH-LEAFY].[BLANK-LINE]Tiles can also be affected by [Weather|WEATHER] and become [FLOODED] or [SCORCHED]. During the winter a Tile's base Terrain usually becomes [Cold|COLD], and may even turn into [SNOW].
Water is an impassable [Terrain|TERRAIN] type. [SETTLEMENT:S] next to large enough bodies of water can build [GALLEY:S].

Rivers are a major [Terrain|TERRAIN] barrier. Normally, crossing Small Rivers is difficult and Large Rivers is impossible, but Rivers can freeze in the winter and become as easy to cross as flat ground.
Roads allow [Armies|ARMY] to ignore any [Terrain|TERRAIN]-based and [Seasonal|SEASON] [Movement|MOVE-POINT] penalties associated with a [Tile|TILE].

The temperature of [Tiles|TILE] change throughout the year and in the winter they will usually become Cold. This shuts down the [Production|PRODUCE] of [Plants|PLANT] by [Farms|FARM] and [Foragers|FORAGE], and usually forces you to subsist off of the [FOOD] you've [Stored|STORED-TURN] earlier in the year.

The temperature of [Tiles|TILE] change throughout the year and in the winter they will usually become Cold. This shuts down the [Production|PRODUCE] of [Plants|PLANT] by [Farms|FARM] and [Foragers|FORAGE], and usually forces you to subsist off of the [FOOD] you've [Stored|STORED-TURN] earlier in the year.

Extreme Weather can affect [Tiles|TILE] in a variety of ways. In the summer particularly hot Tiles can become [SCORCHED], in the winter [BLIZZARD:S] can strike, and throughout the year [RAIN] can strike and when particularly intense Tiles can become [FLOODED].
A Unit is a [Clan|CLAN] in an [Active|ACTIVE] [Profession|PROFESSION] which can [Move|MOVE-POINT] around between map [Tiles|TILE].
A 'Stack' is a group of [Units|UNIT] that move and act together.
Armies are groups of [Clans|CLAN] in [Active|ACTIVE] [Professions|PROFESSION] which can [Move|MOVE-POINT] around between map [Tiles|TILE].

Structures are [Constructed|CONSTRUCT] by [Clans|CLAN] in [Active|ACTIVE] [Professions|PROFESSION], usually to [Harvest|HARVEST] [Resources|RESOURCE] of some kind present on a map [Tile|TILE].[BLANK-LINE]Structures are much better at [Producing|PRODUCE] [Resources|RESOURCE] than [Foragers|FORAGE], and should be prioritized as soon as [TIMBER] and other sources of more advanced [Structure|STRUCTURE]-based [Resources|RESOURCE] are available.[BLANK-LINE]Advanced versions [Constructed|CONSTRUCT] from [STONE-BLOCKS] can be assigned [Apprentices|APPRENTICE] to further increase their [Production|PRODUCE].

All playable [Factions|FACTION] own only a single [SETTLEMENT], which serves as their Capital. Each of the two [Romans|ROME] Factions owns multiple Cities, one of which is their Capital (Rome for the West and Constantinople for the East). Capturing a Playable Faction's Capital is one way to unlock it so you can play as them in future games.

Deposits are sources of [Resources|RESOURCE] you can [Harvest|HARVEST] with [Forager Units|FORAGE] like the [GATHERER], or by more advanced [Structures|STRUCTURE] like the [WHEAT-FARM-1]. All Deposits are visible at the start of a game, although you won't know the [identity|UNIDENTIFIED] of most of them.[BLANK-LINE][Foragers|FORAGE] and basic [Structures|STRUCTURE] constructed from [TIMBER] slowly [Degrade|DEGRADE] the Resources they're Harvesting, but those constructed from [STONE-BLOCKS] do not, making them very valuable long-term investments.[BLANK-LINE]There are 3 types of Deposits in AtG:[BLANK-LINE][*][Plants|PLANT][END-LINE][*][Animals|ANIMAL][END-LINE][*][Minerals|MINERAL]

Deserted Locations can provide bonuses like free [Resources|RESOURCE], but only if investigated by [EXPLORER:A/AN]. It's also possible for some types to hide hidden danger, like [Bandits|BANDIT], so don't be too reckless!

Farms are a group of [Plant|PLANT]-[Producing|PRODUCE] [Structures|STRUCTURE] that include [WHEAT-FARM-1:S], [BARLEY-FARM-1:S], [FRUIT-FARM-1:S], [FLAX-FARM-1:S], [OLIVE-FARM-1:S], and [VINEYARD-1:S].

Mines are a group of [Mineral|MINERAL]-[Producing|PRODUCE] [Structures|STRUCTURE] that include [IRON-MINE-1:S], [COAL-MINE-1:S], [PEAT-MINE-1:S], [GOLD-MINE-1:S], [GEM-MINE-1:S], [SALT-MINE-1:S], and [STONE-QUARRY-1:S].

Pastures are a group of [Structures|STRUCTURE] which add [Animals|ANIMAL] to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] that include [CATTLE-PASTURE-1:S], [SHEEP-PASTURE-1:S], [PIG-PASTURE-1:S], and [HORSE-PASTURE-1:S].[BLANK-LINE]Pastures are [Constructed|CONSTRUCT] by [RANCHER:S].

Plants are one of the three types of [Deposits|DEPOSIT] in At the Gates ([Animals|ANIMAL] and [Minerals|MINERAL] being the other two).[BLANK-LINE]Like all Deposits, Plants can be [Harvested|HARVEST] to [Produce|PRODUCE] [Resources|RESOURCE], but most types must first be [Identified|UNIDENTIFIED] by a [GATHERER], [REAPER], or [SURVEYOR].[BLANK-LINE]There are 7 types of Plants in AtG:[BLANK-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-BERRIES:S] (always Identified)[END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-FRUIT-TREES:S][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-WHEAT:S] (always Identified)[END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-BARLEY:S][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-OLIVES:S][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-GRAPES:S][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-FLAX:S]

Minerals are one of the three types of [Deposits|DEPOSIT] in At the Gates ([Animals|ANIMAL] and [Plants|PLANT] being the other two).[BLANK-LINE]Like all Deposits, Minerals can be [Harvested|HARVEST] to [Produce|PRODUCE] [Resources|RESOURCE], but all types must first be [Identified|UNIDENTIFIED] by a [DIGGER] or [SURVEYOR].[BLANK-LINE]There are 7 types of Minerals in AtG:[BLANK-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-IRON][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-STONE][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-COAL][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-PEAT][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-SALT][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-GOLD][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-GEMS]

Animals are one of the three types of [Deposits|DEPOSIT] in At the Gates ([Plants|PLANT] and [Minerals|MINERAL] being the other two).[BLANK-LINE]Like all Deposits, Animals can be [Harvested|HARVEST] to [Produce|PRODUCE] [Resources|RESOURCE], but most types must first be [Identified|UNIDENTIFIED] by a [HUNTER], [TRAPPER], or [SURVEYOR].[BLANK-LINE]All types of Animals can be [Harvested|HARVEST] for [MEAT] by a [HUNTER] and [PARCHMENT] or [CLOTH] (as leather) by a [TRAPPER]. All Animals but [DEER] can be added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] and used by advanced [Professions|PROFESSION] like the [MEAT-CUTTER] and [PARCHMENT-MAKER].[BLANK-LINE]There are 5 types of Animals in AtG:[BLANK-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-DEER] (always Identified)[END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-HORSES][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-CATTLE][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-SHEEP][END-LINE][*][DEPOSIT-PIGS]

Most [Deposits|DEPOSIT] start off Unidentified and you must have a [Clan|CLAN] in an appropriate [Profession|PROFESSION] figure out what it is before you can use it.[BLANK-LINE][Plants|PLANT] can be identified by [GATHERER:S] and [REAPER:S], [Minerals|MINERAL] by [DIGGER:S], and [Animals|ANIMAL] by [HUNTER:S] and [TRAPPER:S]. Deposits of any kind can be identified by the [SURVEYOR].
When the game starts you have a bit of information about your surroundings in the form of 'Rumors'. These can help shape your early strategy before you've had a chance to explore more directly.

Each [Clan|CLAN] can be [Trained|TRAIN] in one Profession, which can be [Active|ACTIVE] or [Settled|SETTLED] and grant it the ability to [Harvest|HARVEST] or refine [Resources|RESOURCE], explore the map, [Fight|WARRIOR] enemies, and more.[BLANK-LINE]The performance of Clans in a specific Profession can be improved by buying [Upgrades|UPGRADE] for it. Note that these Upgrades only apply for that specific Profession - switch to something else and the effect will be lost.

There are six [Disciplines|DISCIPLINE] which [Professions|PROFESSION] and [Clans|CLAN] can belong to: [HONOR], [AGRICULTURE], [LIVESTOCK], [METALWORKING], [CRAFTING], and [DISCOVERY].[BLANK-LINE]Clans gain [Experience|XP] and can increase their [Level|LEVEL] in the Discipline their Profession belongs to, which reduces the [Turns|TURN] needed to [Train|TRAIN] it in more advanced Professions within that Discipline.

[Clans|CLAN] increase in Level within a [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] based on how much [XP|XP] they have.[BLANK-LINE]As long as a Clan remains in a Profession within the same Discipline it will continue gaining Levels, but switching to a Profession in a different Discipline will reset that Clan back to Level 0.[BLANK-LINE]Upon reaching Level 10 a Clan can acquire 1 [Upgrade|UPGRADE] for free (the Upgrade must still first have been [Studied|LEARN]).[BLANK-LINE][Learning|LEARN] the initial Discipline [Tech|TECH] for each branch of the Tech Tree allows you to pick one of your Clans to give 2 free Levels in that Discipline, giving you a head start on [Training|TRAIN] them in associated [Professions|PROFESSION].

[Clans|CLAN] gain 1 Experience Point (or XP) in a [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] each turn they spend in a [Profession|PROFESSION] belonging to that Discipline. Upon reaching the required amount of XP a Clan will [Level Up|LEVEL]. This threshold increases with each Level.

in Active [Professions|PROFESSION] leave the [SETTLEMENT] and appear on the map (as opposed to [Settled|SETTLED] Professions). Some Clans have Traits that make them prefer or dislike such Professions.
in Settled [Professions|PROFESSION] remain inside the [SETTLEMENT] (as opposed to [Active|ACTIVE] Professions). Some Clans have Traits that make them prefer or dislike such Professions.
[Clans|CLAN] become the Resident of a [Structure|STRUCTURE] after they finish [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] it, or when they join it as an [Apprentice|APPRENTICE].

Advanced [STONE-BLOCKS:SINGULAR]-based [Structures|STRUCTURE] last forever, but their [Production|PRODUCE] can also be greatly increased by adding a [Clan|CLAN] in the same [Profession|PROFESSION] as the Clan which originally built it (e.g. more [FARMER:S] can be added to [Farms|FARM].[BLANK-LINE]To add an Apprentice to a Structure must have an available 'Apprentice Slot'. Structures start with 0, and you must spend [BOARDS] to add slots. Assigning an actual Clan then requires spending [PARCHMENT]. Apprentices can be an expensive investment, but allow you to make your most valuable Structures incredibly productive.

Ennobling a [Clan|CLAN] costs 10 [FINE-CLOTH] and 10 [ARMOR].[BLANK-LINE]Once Ennobled, a Clan permanently gains a +1 [Mood|MOOD] bonus, [Produces|PRODUCE] +5 [FAME] per turn, and immediately gains 5 [Levels|LEVEL] in a [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] of your choice.[BLANK-LINE]Furthermore, some advanced [Professions|PROFESSION] (such as the [HEAVY-CAVALRY]) are themselves 'Noble', meaning that only Clans which have been Ennobled may be [Trained|TRAIN] in them.

Some [Professions|PROFESSION] are considered 'Social', which can affect how long it takes to [Train|TRAIN] [Clans|CLAN] in them or alter the effectiveness of a Clan, based on that Clan's [Traits|CLAN-TRAIT]. Most Clans don't need to care about this, but some do, so pay attention. Some Clans will actual refuse to work in Social Professions entirely![BLANK-LINE]Social Professions can be recognized by the
icon, and are exclusively found in the [HONOR] and [DISCOVERY] [Disciplines|DISCIPLINE].

Warrior [Professions|PROFESSION] are [Active|ACTIVE] Professions which can fight one another (as opposed to [Civilian|CIVILIAN] Professions). Warriors can also capture Civilian [Units|UNIT], and capture or pillage [Structures|STRUCTURE].

Civilian [Professions|PROFESSION] are those which do not involve combat or fighting (as opposed to [Warrior|WARRIOR] Professions). Most are [Settled|SETTLED], but a few are [Active|ACTIVE]. Most involve economics or [Resources|RESOURCE] in some way.

Cavalry [Units|UNIT] are expensive but powerful [Warriors|WARRIOR] which have a large amount of [Move Points|MOVE-POINT] and require [WARHORSES] to [Train|TRAIN]. Aside from their expense, the primary drawback of Cavalry is that they require 2 [Supply|SUPPLY] from their [Tile|TILE], rather than just 1 like [Infantry|INFANTRY] Units.

Infantry [Units|UNIT] are the basic [Warriors|WARRIOR] of AtG. They're not especially fancy, but they get the job done. They only require 1 [Supply|SUPPLY] from their [Tile|TILE], making them more flexible than their expensive [Cavalry|CAVALRY] counterparts.

A Clan is a group of [Families|FAMILY] which act as a single unit. Each Clan can be [Trained|TRAIN] in a [Profession|PROFESSION] in order to grant it the ability to [Harvest|HARVEST] or refine [Resources|RESOURCE], explore the map, [Fight|WARRIOR] enemies, and more. You will usually end up with around 40-50 Clans over the course of a single game.

Each [Clan|CLAN] is made up of one or more Families. Each [Consumes|CONSUME] 0.1 [FOOD] per [Turn|TURN] (more on higher Difficulty Levels) and a new one joins every six months (12 Turns). As a game progresses your Food needs will continue accelerating, so always be thinking about how you're going to keep all of those hungry mouths fed![BLANK-LINE]Each additional Family which joins a Clan provides a 5% bonus to [Resource Production|PRODUCE] and [Combat Power|POWER], so while old, large Clans can be hard to feed they can also be quite useful in important roles.

Every [Clan|CLAN] has two Traits which shape which [Professions|PROFESSION] it's well-suited for, what kind of [Desires|DESIRE] it might develop, and generally how you'll want to use and manage it.[BLANK-LINE]Some Clans already start in Professions, making them useful but maybe hard to manage later when they don't want to switch [Disciplines|DISCIPLINE]. Other Clans might be inclined to commit [Crimes|CRIME], encouraging you to isolate them so they don't cause annoy anyone. There are around 100 Traits in AtG, so the number of possible combinations is quite large.

A [Clan's|CLAN] Mood is normally 'Content' but this can change if they develop a [Desire|DESIRE] or have a [Trait|CLAN-TRAIT] which alters their default Mood somehow.[BLANK-LINE]A happy Clan receives a bonus to [Resource Production|PRODUCE] and [Morale|MORALE], whereas unhappy Clans suffer a penalty to these.[BLANK-LINE]Clans can temporarily be made happier (and thus productive) by spending Alcohol. A Clan's Mood drops when it has an unsatisfied [Desire|DESIRE], or when sharing a [Tile|TILE] with another Clan which has committed a [Crime|CRIME].

[Clans|CLAN] can develop a Desire based on their [Traits|CLAN-TRAIT], for example a Clan with the 'Easily Cold' Trait will likely [become unhappy|MOOD] if [Trained|TRAIN] as an [EXPLORER] and sent off during the winter.[BLANK-LINE]The longer a Clan has a Desire which hasn't been fulfilled the more their [Mood|MOOD] can drop. Ignoring a Clan's Desire can be done, but this is generally not advised. Satisfying a Clan's Desire will result in it receiving a permanent Mood bonus.[BLANK-LINE][Feuds|FEUD] are a special type of Desire where two Clans decide they hate each other and want you to [Punish|PUNISH] the other. Once a Feud has begun you will likely need to intervene.[BLANK-LINE]Most Clans will never develop a Desire, so this isn't something you'll need to spend too much time or energy dealing with. Pay attention to your Clans' Traits though, as some especially troublesome Clans are usually pretty easy to spot!

Feuds are a special kind of [Desire|DESIRE] that two [Clans|CLAN] develop together. When this occurs both Clans will start dropping in [Mood|MOOD] until you [Punish|PUNISH] one of them. Most of the time it makes the most strategic sense to Punish the less-useful Clan, but what you choose to do is a question of conscience for you alone!

[Clans|CLAN] with certain negative [Traits|CLAN-TRAIT] like 'Aggressive' or 'Rowdy' can sometimes commit a Crime. This doesn't have any tangible effect other than making all other Clans on their tile [unhappy|MOOD].[BLANK-LINE]The negative effects of having committed one or more Crimes can be eliminated by [Punishing|PUNISH] the offending Clan. Another option is to plan ahead and actively shuffle potentially troublesome Clans off into [Active|ACTIVE] Professions away from other Clans.

You can Punish a [Clan|CLAN] in your [SETTLEMENT] after it's committed a [Crime|CRIME] or become involved in a [Feud|FEUD]. Doing so settles the matter and any associated penalties, but strips the Clan of their [Profession|PROFESSION] and [Discipline|DISCIPLINE], and gives them a permanent [Mood|MOOD] penalty.

[Units|UNIT] and [SETTLEMENT:S] can move around the map, the rate being determined by what types of [Terrain|TERRAIN] they move through. The base cost to move into a [Tile|TILE] is 1, but a [HILL] adds one extra, while a [Forest|FOREST] consumes them all.
Vision is the ability to see what's on a [Tile|TILE]. Tiles in and next to your [Borders|BORDERS] are visible, as are those near your [Units|UNIT]. Some Units can see further, like [EXPLORER:S].

Supply represents the basic goods needed to keep a [Unit|UNIT] functioning. During the summer most [Clans|CLAN] will be able to freely collect what they need from their [Tile|TILE], but is no longer the case once it becomes [Cold|COLD]. At this point a Unit may need to [Encamp|ENCAMP] to avoid taking [Damage|DAMAGE]. Some [Units|UNIT], like the [LIGHT-CAVALRY], actually require more than 1 Supply Point, so always keep an eye on what a Tile actually provides!
[GALLEY:S] are able to transport your [SETTLEMENT] or one [Unit|UNIT] at a time over the water as 'Cargo', typically much faster than is possible by land.
Power is a rating of a [Unit|UNIT]/[Army|ARMY]'s military strength. One Army with double the Power of another will beat it up pretty good but not quite kill it.
When on offense your goal is to reduce the defender's
to 0 as this causes them to [Retreat|RETREAT] to an adjacent
, and inflicts a significant amount of
are usually won by the defender, so always try to outnumber your enemy.[BLANK-LINE]Attacking

does not automatically regenerate. Before striking, make sure you're strong enough punch through and drive the enemy back![BLANK-LINE]Defenders who [Dig In|DIG-IN] and on rough [Terrain|TERRAIN] (such as a [Forest|FOREST]) will receive a defensive bonus.
When on defense the goal is to hold out and and keep your
from dropping to 0 as this causes your [Army|ARMY] to [Retreat|RETREAT] to an adjacent
, and inflicts a significant amount of
are usually won by the defender (in part thanks to their ability to [Dig In|DIG-IN], so as long as you have numbers and [Supply|SUPPLY] on your side you'll be fine.[BLANK-LINE]Attacking

Each [Clan|CLAN] has 100 Health points. As they suffer Damage their [Combat Power|POWER] decreases (50% Damage = 50% Power).[BLANK-LINE]Damage can be Healed in the [SETTLEMENT] by spending [OIL] and [CLOTH].

Each [Clan|CLAN] has 100 Health points. As they suffer Damage their [Combat Power|POWER] decreases (50% Damage = 50% Power).[BLANK-LINE]Damage can be Healed in the [SETTLEMENT] by spending [OIL] and [CLOTH].

[Warrior|WARRIOR] [Units|UNIT] each have 100 Morale points, though this can be increased by gaining [Combat Experience|COMBAT-XP] or modified by a [Clan's|CLAN] [Traits|CLAN-TRAIT]. When an [Army|ARMY]'s Morale is reduced to 0 they to Retreat to an adjacent
and suffer a significant amount of
When an [Army|ARMY]'s Morale is reduced to 0 they to Retreat to an adjacent
and suffer a significant amount of
The number of Combat Experience points a [Warrior|WARRIOR] has earned are added to its [Morale|MORALE] stat, making it tougher and less inclined to [Retreat|RETREAT].

Leaders which share the same Religion receive a [Relationship Level|RL] bonus, while those who do not receive a penalty (the exact amount being determined by each Leader's [Traits|LEADER-TRAIT]). Switching Religions can be a good way of making friends, but also enemies - abandoning the faith of another Leader can make them especially upset.

Repuation is a Leader's global 'diplomatic currency' that can be spent on future diplomatic interactions with ANY Leader in order to get them to do more than they would normally be willing.[BLANK-LINE]Reputation is a valuable commodity hard to earn - doing so usually involves great risk, sacrifice, or boldness.

Influence is a Leader's 'diplomatic currency' that can be spent on future diplomatic interactions with the Leader it was earned with in order to get them to do more than they would normally be willing.[BLANK-LINE]Influence is gained in equal amount any time a Leader's [Relationship Level|RL] increases.

Relationship Level is a metric measuring how much one Leader likes another.[BLANK-LINE]Having a strong Relationship Level will make another Leader less likely to attack and more inclined to positive diplomatic interactions, but important actions will also often require spending [Influence|INFLUENCE] or [Reputation|REPUTATION].[BLANK-LINE]A Leader's Relationship Level generally starts at 0 and can range from -10 to +10.

Leverage is the sum total of [Relationship Level|RL], [Influence|INFLUENCE], and [Reputation|REPUTATION] being spent or used as part of one possible diplomatic exchange. Influence and Reputation can be held back, in which case they don't contribute to the overall Leverage score.
You can spend an Emissary via the Diplomacy Screen with any [Leader|LEADER] to improve your [Relationship Level|RL] with them. Emissaries are acquired by [producing|PRODUCE] [PRESTIGE].

An Interaction is a single exchange between two Leaders. It might be a greeting, a threat, or a gift with a message. The types of Interactions an AI Leader chooses is based on their [Approach|APPROACH].
Each Leader has an Approach, which determines their diplomatic behavior. The Approach they choose is based on your [Relationship Level|RL] and their [Personality Traits|LEADER-TRAIT].

A Leader can be Friends with other Leaders, which affects their behavior towards them. Being nice to a Leader's Friends can improve your relationship with the Leader, while being mean to them can hurt it.

A Leader can be Enemies with other Leaders, which affects their behavior towards them. Being nice to a Leader's Enemies can hurt your relationship with the Leader, while being mean to them can help improve it.

An Alliance is a permanent diplomatic arrangement which binds two [Factions|FACTION] together, and from that point on all wars either is engaged in will be joined by the other. A [Leader|LEADER] will agree to an Alliance if you have at least 5 [Leverage|LEVERAGE] with them.[BLANK-LINE]Forming an Alliance with a Faction is one of the two ways you can unlock that Faction for play in future games (the other being to conquer their [Capital|CAPITAL]).

Resources are the economic currency of AtG, and in conjunction with [Clans|CLAN] are essential to doing just about anything.[BLANK-LINE]There are two basic kinds of Resources: raw and refined. An example of a raw Resource is [IRON], which is [Harvested|HARVEST] from [Deposits|DEPOSIT] but can't actually be used for anything until refined into [WEAPONS] or [TOOLS].[BLANK-LINE]The most important Resource in the game is [FOOD], as without it your Clans will [Starve|STARVE]. [FAME] determines how often new Clans join you. [KNOWLEDGE] determines how fast you can [Study|STUDY] new [Professions|PROFESSION]. [TREASURE] is a versatile Resource that is especially useful for buying other Resources from [Caravans|CARAVAN]. [TIMBER] is required to [Construct|CONSTRUCT] basic [Structures|STRUCTURE], while [STONE-BLOCKS] are needed for more advanced versions which don't ever [Degrade|DEGRADE] and run out.

[Resource|RESOURCE] production is a fundamental part of At the Gates.[BLANK-LINE]Initially you will simply [Harvest|HARVEST] them from [Deposits|DEPOSIT] with [Foragers|FORAGE] or buy them from the [Caravan|CARAVAN], but once your tribe is a bit more developed you'll be able to start refining raw Resources like [IRON] into other things like [TOOLS] with the help of [Professions|PROFESSION] like the [BLACKSMITH].[BLANK-LINE]Because you have a limited number of [Clans|CLAN] it's usually best to prioritize the production of one or two Resources rather than try to do everything.

[Resources|RESOURCE] can be consumed each [Turn|TURN] in two ways:[BLANK-LINE][*]Resources like [FOOD] are automatically consumed based on the number of [Clan|CLAN] [Families|FAMILY] you have.[BLANK-LINE][*]Raw Resources like [IRON] can be refined into more advanced things like [TOOLS] and are consumed in the process.[BLANK-LINE]The consumption of Resources by individual Clans can be disabled by clicking on that Clan in the Clans Screen and then clicking on the 'Stop Work' button. The consumption of 'special' [FOOD] Resources like [OLIVES] and [GRAPES] can be disabled by mousing over that Resource in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] and then clicking the 'Disable Substitution for Food' button.

You can [Produce|PRODUCE] raw [Resources|RESOURCE] from [Plant|PLANT] and [Mineral|MINERAL] [Deposits|DEPOSIT] in two ways: having a [Clan|CLAN] in certain [Professions|PROFESSION] [Forage|FORAGE] it (e.g. [DIGGER]), or by [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] certain [Structures|STRUCTURE] on it (e.g. [IRON-MINE-1]).[BLANK-LINE]Harvesting a Deposit gradually [Depletes|DEGRADE] and eventually exhausts it, after which it can no longer be Harvested. Nearly all Deposits can be Harvested for 18-24 [Turns|TURN] before running out (regardless of how much is actually being Produced) with the exact figure being determined by its type (e.g. 18 for [DEPOSIT-BEEHIVE:A/AN], 24 for [DEPOSIT-IRON:A/AN]).[BLANK-LINE][Animal Herds|ANIMAL] are the third type of Deposit, and function differently. Instead of being Harvested directly like Plants and Minerals Animals can either be Foraged by [HUNTER:A/AN] or [TRAPPER] to Produce [MEAT] or [PARCHMENT], or they can be added to your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] by Constructing a [Pasture|PASTURE] on them.[BLANK-LINE]Animals in your Stockpile can be eaten directly for much more [FOOD] than a Hunter provides, and can also be used by [Settled|SETTLED] Professions like the [MEAT-CUTTER] and [PARCHMENT-MAKER] which Produce even more than that.

Your stockpile of [Resources|RESOURCE] is visible on the left side of the World Screen. Once 'in the bank' you can save Resources for future purposes, or trade them away at the [Caravan|CARAVAN] for other Resources you need.[BLANK-LINE][FOOD] is a special type of Resource that other kinds of Resources like [WHEAT] and [GRAPES] automatically turn into when [Produced|PRODUCE]. [FOOD] also enters your stockpile as ['Stored Turns'|STORED-TURN], rather than a specific quantity like other Resources.

If you have at least 24 [FOOD] in your [Stockpile|STOCKPILE] then a percentage of any further [Production|PRODUCE] will start to 'spoil'. The initial penalthy is -50%, and every every additional 12 [FOOD] another half of the remaining Production will be lost. Storing up large quantities of [FOOD] can provide security but isn't the most efficient way to run your economy.

[Harvesting|HARVEST] a [Deposit|DEPOSIT] or [Dense Forest|FOREST] gradually Depletes and eventually exhausts it, after which it can no longer be Harvested. Nearly all Deposits can be Harvested for 18-24 [Turns|TURN] before running out (regardless of how much is actually being [Produced|PRODUCE] from it) with the exact figure being determined by its type (e.g. 18 for [DEPOSIT-BEEHIVE:A/AN], 24 for [DEPOSIT-IRON:A/AN]).

[Harvesting|HARVEST] a [Deposit|DEPOSIT] gradually Depletes and eventually exhausts it, after which it can no longer be Harvested. Nearly all Deposits can be Harvested for 18-24 [Turns|TURN] before running out (regardless of how much is actually being [Produced|PRODUCE] from it) with the exact figure being determined by its type (e.g. 18 for [DEPOSIT-BEEHIVE:A/AN], 24 for [DEPOSIT-IRON:A/AN]).

[Harvesting|HARVEST] a [Deposit|DEPOSIT] gradually Depletes and eventually exhausts it, after which it can no longer be Harvested. Nearly all Deposits can be Harvested for 18-24 [Turns|TURN] before running out (regardless of how much is actually being [Produced|PRODUCE] from it) with the exact figure being determined by its type (e.g. 18 for [DEPOSIT-BEEHIVE:A/AN], 24 for [DEPOSIT-IRON:A/AN]).

A [Structure|STRUCTURE] which normally [Harvests|HARVEST] [Resources|RESOURCE] can go 'offline' and stop doing under a few different circumstances:[BLANK-LINE][*]Most [FOOD] production stops when it gets [Cold|COLD].[BLANK-LINE][*]Random [Weather|WEATHER] effects can temporarily shut down a Structure.[BLANK-LINE][*]A Structure goes offline after it's been [Pillaged|PILLAGE].

When an enemy [Structure|STRUCTURE] or [Clan|CLAN] is captured from another [Faction|FACTION] it is 'Occupied' for 6 turns, and during this time it will no longer [Harvest|HARVEST] [Resources|RESOURCE].

[Clans|CLAN] and [Structures|STRUCTURE] that are captured from another [Faction|FACTION] suffer a permanent -50% penalty to [Resource Production|PRODUCE] and [Morale|MORALE]. Captured Clans have a red-tinted card background.

If your Tribe runs out of [FOOD] all of your [Clans'|CLAN] [Mood|MOOD] will drop by 1 per turn. If more than half of your clans become Resentful you will be overthrown and lose the game![BLANK-LINE]Each of your [Clans'|CLAN] [Families|FAMILY] [Consumes|CONSUME] 0.2 per [Turn|TURN], so make sure you have enough to last through the winter!
Your Tribe can only support a certain number of

, and once you reach this limit you will no longer gain any [FAME].[BLANK-LINE]You can increase your Clan Limit by clicking the 'Increase Clan Limit' button in the Clans Screen and spending [CLOTH]. The cost of this action increases each time you use it.

Your [SETTLEMENT] is able to Train one [Clan|CLAN] at a time, and usually in a [Profession|PROFESSION]. Professions grant a Clan the ability to [Harvest|HARVEST] or refine [Resources|RESOURCE], explore the map, [Fight|WARRIOR] enemies, and more.[BLANK-LINE]You can also Train Clans in a specific [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] by clicking on the 'Train Clan in Discipline' button in the Clans Screen. This can be a good way to get a Clan ready for a role you know you want it to serve in but don't have the Resources or have finished [Studying|STUDY] the target Profession yet. Switching a Clan from one Discipline to another is free, and you can further increase its [Level|LEVEL] in a Discipline this way, but this requires spending [PARCHMENT].[BLANK-LINE]If there's nothing you want to Train during the turn you can click the 'Skip' button in the bottom-left corner of the Clans Screen in order to finish the turn.

in some [Active|ACTIVE] [Civilian|CIVILIAN] [Professions|PROFESSION] like [FARMER] and [MINER] can Construct [Structures|STRUCTURE] to [Harvest|HARVEST] [Resources|RESOURCE] from [Deposits|DEPOSIT] or [Dense Forests|FOREST]. This requires either [TIMBER] or [STONE-BLOCKS], and is generally not something you'll start doing until a year or two into the game.

You can Study one [Tech|TECH] (usually a [Profession|PROFESSION]) at a time, and once [Learned|LEARN] you can [Train|TRAIN] your [Clans|CLAN] in it.[BLANK-LINE]The rate at which you Learn a Tech is based on its [KNOWLEDGE] cost, and how much [KNOWLEDGE] you're [Producing|PRODUCE] each turn.[BLANK-LINE]To Study more advanced Professions at least one Profession from the previous 'tier' in its [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] tree must have already been Learned.

Once you've [Studied|STUDY] a [Tech|TECH] long enough to accumulate the full [KNOWLEDGE] cost you may then make use of it:[BLANK-LINE][*]After Learning a [Profession|PROFESSION] you may then [Train|TRAIN] [Clans|CLAN] in it.[BLANK-LINE][*]After Learning an [Upgrade|UPGRADE] you can then spend the [Resources|RESOURCE] required to apply it to  a Clan in the associated Profession.[BLANK-LINE][*]Each of the 6 [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] Techs you can Study at the start of the game allows you to give one of your Clans 2 free [Levels|LEVEL] in that Discipline.

There are 3 kinds of Techs which you can [Study|STUDY] and [Learn|LEARN]:[BLANK-LINE][*][Professions|PROFESSION][BLANK-LINE][*][Upgrades|UPGRADE][BLANK-LINE][*][Discipline|DISCIPLINE] [Level-Up|LEVEL] Techs

Most [Professions|PROFESSION] have 2 associated Upgrade [Techs|TECH] you can [Learn|LEARN] and then purchase for [Clans|CLAN] in that Profession. The [Resource|RESOURCE] cost of each Upgrade is different, so pay attention! Upgrades only apply for that specific Profession - switch to something else and the effect will be lost.[BLANK-LINE]Note that a Profession's Upgrade Techs can be [Studied|STUDY] in either  order - you need not finish one before the other, despite how they're stacked in the screen.[BLANK-LINE]Upon reaching [Level|LEVEL] 10 in a [Discipline|DISCIPLINE] a Clan can be given one free Upgrade.
Foraging is an action which allows

in some [Active|ACTIVE] [Civilian|CIVILIAN] [Professions|PROFESSION] like [GATHERER] and [HUNTER] to [Harvest|HARVEST] [Resources|RESOURCE] from [Deposits|DEPOSIT] or [Dense Forests|FOREST] without the need for a [Structure|STRUCTURE]. Foraging is generally a less efficient way of [Producing|PRODUCE] Resources though, so once you've [Learned|LEARN] Professions capable of [Constructing|CONSTRUCT] Structures and have the [TIMBER] to do so it's probably time to switch over.

A [Warrior|WARRIOR] can be given the 'Dig In' command, which allows it to accumulate a [Defense|DEFENSE] bonus on its current [Tile|TILE] of 25% per turn, up to a maximum of 100%.[BLANK-LINE]This bonus will be lost as soon as the [Unit|UNIT] moves or performs another action, so don't expect to go anywhere for a while after you Dig In.[BLANK-LINE]Digging In does not provide a Unit with [Supply|SUPPLY], making this a very dangerous action to perform outside your [Borders|BORDERS] during the [winter|COLD].

, which is invaluable during the [winter|SEASON]. A strategy you will often employ during the cold months is to alternate between Encamping on one turn to replenish your Supply and then perform whatever action you were in the middle of on the next (e.g. [Foraging|FORAGE] or [identifying Deposits|UNIDENTIFIED].[BLANK-LINE]This action can only be performed if a Unit hasn't yet spent any
. When you order a Unit to Encamp it will automatically wake up the next

Pillaging takes 1 [Turn|TURN] and provides a lump sum of [Resources|RESOURCE] as plunder, but damages the target [Structure|STRUCTURE] until it's Repaired. Pillaging a [Farm|FARM] or [SETTLEMENT] also provides free
.[BLANK-LINE]Starving the enemy into submission can reduce or eliminate the need for a bloody frontal [Attack|ATTACK]. However, the enemy
always has the option to withdraw and break the

at any time. Adding a [CATAPULT] to a Siege can greatly accelerate the process.[BLANK-LINE]A Siege can only be performed by an Army which outnumbers its opponent (in terms of number of [Warrior Units|WARRIOR]).
Packing Up your [SETTLEMENT] allows it to [Move|MOVE-POINT] around the map like a [Unit|UNIT]. This takes 1 full
.[BLANK-LINE]While your Settlement is Packed Up you will no longer be able to

in a [Profession|PROFESSION] or [Discipline|DISCIPLINE], however with at least 1 Move Point remaining it may be Unpacked at any time.[BLANK-LINE]Moving your Settlement allows you to shift which [Tiles|TILE] are in your [Borders|BORDERS], making it possible to [Construct|CONSTRUCT] [Structures|STRUCTURE] on nearby [Deposits|DEPOSIT] and extending how far you receive free [Supply|SUPPLY].

You can spend [WEAPONS] to Fortify your [SETTLEMENT] or any [Structure|STRUCTURE] and increase the range your [Borders|BORDERS] extend from it by 1 ring of [Tiles|TILE]. The cost of doing so increases each time, but this can be a way to reach one or two specific, important [Deposits|DEPOSIT] you just need to have.+

Declaring your Tribe as a Kingdom costs 25 [PARCHMENT] and permanently fixes your [SETTLEMENT] onto its current [Tile|TILE], but offers some significant bonuses in return (+2 [Border Range|BORDERS] for your [SETTLEMENT] and +15 [FAME] per turn).[BLANK-LINE]Because you'll no longer be able to [move|MOVE-POINT] your Settlement after Declaring a Kingdom make sure you're in a good place you can continue to expand from before doing so![BLANK-LINE]As a general rule of thumb you won't want to perform this action until you're at least 50-75 turns into the game.

Patch notes may not contain [ or ] (square brackets), as this is reserved syntax for the text tag lookup system and will crash the game when the patch notes are shown.
Unable to open or locate  [ Settings.xml ]  from the expected location:

[ Settings.xml ]  is corrupted and cannot be loaded. It will now be regenerated, which will likely reset previously-assigned user settings. A copy of the corrupted file will also be made to aid in fixing and/or recovering from this error.

File Location:
This file contains all of the game's customizable settings.
It will eventually be possible to change settings in-game, but for now they must be edited by hand.
* This file is overwritten every time the game launches or changes a setting internally (e.g. when the grid is toggled).
* To restore any setting to the default simply delete the line and relaunch the game.
* After a clean install all YES/NO settings default to [no].
Launch the game in a borderless window (0), fullscreen (1) or window (2)
* * *  WARNING  * * *  Right now, setting this to fullscreen can lower the framerate considerably and may cause the game to crash. It is recommended you use borderless window setting (0) instead.
]. Maximum is this computer's current desktop width of [
]. Maximum is this computer's current desktop height of [
Mutes all of the game audio
Disable all game audio.   *** NOTE ***   Try changing this to YES if your game is crashing on launch or when starting a game.
Mute the music which starts playing when the game is launched.
Mute the WORLD AMBIENCE (birds chirping, wind blowing, etc.).
Mute the soft 'tap' sound effect played when a tooltip appears.
The volume of the music that starts playing in the main menu.   Must be a decimal value between 0 and 1.0.
The volume of the music that plays in the game following an important event.   Must be a decimal value between 0 and 1.0.
The volume the WORLD AMBIENCE is played at (birds chirping, wind blowing, etc.).   Must be a decimal value between 0 and 1.0.
The volume all SOUND EFFECTS are played at (button clicks, selection confirmations, etc.).   Must be a decimal value between 0 and 1.0.
Enables the drawing of the map grid.
Determines whether and how in-text icons for names and concepts are displayed.  MODES :  TextWithNoIcons, IconsBeforeNames, IconsBeforeNamesAndConcepts, IconsWithNoText
Disables the flashing effect on the End Turn button, Selection Panel Apprentices count label, Clan Screen Unpack button, etc.
Disables the colored banner which appears at the start of every turn.
Disables the transition to strategic view when zoomed out.
Disables camera movement when the cursor is near the edge of the screen.
Defines how many pixels away from the edge of the screen the mouse cursor will start moving the camera (in fullscreen mode this is always 1).
Make the left mouse button act as the right, and vice-versa.
Make the IJKL keys act like WASD.
Turn on 'Supply Range' mode [ Alt-S ] at the start of a game.
Allow the turn to be ended even if Units/Settlements haven't yet been given orders.
Replaces the [Close] button found at the bottom of many screens and popups with an [x] in the top-right corner.
Increases the default button pixel height from 40 to 70 (note that this won't affect EVERY button in the game).
The number of update calls to wait before cycling to the next idle unit.
The number of milliseconds the Notification 'summary' stays up for at the start of a new turn.
How many pixels the Notification 'summary' moves per update when sliding in from the edge of the screen.
The SMALLEST scaling possible when zooming out (1.0 means zoomed in all the way, 0.1 means we back up until we can see 10x as much as normal).
The amount the scaling changes each time the camera is zoomed in or out.
How much information is shown in the Knowledge Screen. 0 = Less, 1 = More, 2 = All
How much information is shown in the Professions Screen. 0 = Less, 1 = More, 2 = All
How much information is shown in the Caravan Screen. 0 = Less, 2 = All
Hides the helper icons displayed beneath the Profession Buttons in the Training and Research Screens.
Show the exact quantity and loss for a deficient Resource, instead of turns remaining.
The map seed of the most recently played Group Game. If not equal to the current seed, the user cannot play on random maps.
The version of AtG that has most recently been played. Used to decide what changelogs to show the user.
Show the 'Welcome to AtG' series of popups after launching the game.
The next game will use the fixed seed intro map, which has been chosen to provide a fairly balanced game experience.
The number of intro maps that have been played, after which totally random seeds are used.
The number of turns between the creation of autosaves. 0 = every turn.
Show a warning on the main menu if the number of save files exceeds this threshold. Increases each time it's hit.
Turn off the 'Guide' and hide all in-game Help Tips.
Remove the restriction where each Guide Help Tip is only shown once, ever.
The list of the Tips provided by the Help Guide and whether each has been shown before (and should not be again).
Remember login settings and automatically login into your account.
Turn on various boring debugging options, like making the Fixed Seed Test Map buttons appear in the main menu.
End Turn button no longer changes to bring up the Knowledge screen, Caravan screen, etc.
Shuts down all combat AI logic (mainly useful in slightly reducing turn processing time).
Shuts down all AI planning logic (mainly useful in seriously reducing turn processing time).
Imposes a timer on each turn for the supplied number of seconds.
Show the game's frames per second in the upper-left corner of the screen.
Show the X,Y position of the mouse cursor in the upper-left corner of the screen.
Skip past the main menu and automatically load the Quicksave. NOTE: Tested BEFORE [QuickstartMapSize].
If a valid Map Size ID (e.g. MAP_SIZE_STANDARD, MAP_SIZE_TINY), skip the main menu and automatically launch a fixed seed map. NOTE: Tested AFTER [QuickloadAfterLaunch].
The rand seed to use every time a fixed seed debug map is created.
If a valid month (e.g. Early January, Late September), force new games to start during that month.
The Tribal Council meets every turn. This not only allows its members to be changed, but shortens the max term limit to just 2 turns.
The Caravan is available every turn.
An AI Leader will contact the Human every turn, if possible.
All players have contact with one another from the start of the game.
The index of the tile to center the camera on at the start of a game. [0] will automatically pick the center tile.
The number of times to zoom out the camera at the start of a game.
Immediately reveal the map and enter vis cheat mode.
Immediately clear the entire map and enter art debug mode.
Immediately turn on the tile debug overlay (normally displayed by holding down ~).
If a valid Sage ID (e.g. SAGE_POPULARITY), force this Luminary to appear when the first group arrives.
Show advanced error messages during map generation.
Makes all Resource Deposits visible and unshrouded.
Don't place ANY objects during map generation.
Place a semi-transparent mask over tiles that would normally be fogged when in vis cheat mode.
Allow the Debug Command Console to be brought up by pressing the grave key (`, AKA the tilde key) or Ctrl-C.
Cycles the size of the Debug Command Console output window. 0 = Tiny, 1 = Normal, 2 = Full
Do we write log output to the console window? (in addition to the log files)
